basic_details.short_information: BRO is recruiting 899 posts across 6 trades with Matriculation/10+2/ITI qualification, last date 04 July 2026 (for most states).

important_dates.exam_date: To be decided by Commandant, GREF Centre, Pune

application_fee.details: Candidates must pay the non-refundable application fee online through the SBI Collect portal only (https://www.onlinesbi.sbi/sbicollect/icollecthome.htm?corpID=1232156) in favour of Commandant, GREF Centre, Pune-411 015. No other mode of payment will be accepted. A copy of the e-receipt must be attached with the application form.

age_limit.details: Age limit for all posts/trades is between 18 to 27 years as on 01 Jun 2026. Age relaxation is applicable as per Government of India guidelines for various categories. Government servants get relaxation up to 40 years (UR) and 45 years (SC/ST). PwBD candidates get 10 years relaxation in upper age limit.

qualification.details: Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the closing date of submission of application. Only Indian National Males are eligible to apply. Candidates awaiting results of final examination need not apply. All educational/technical qualifications must be from recognized University/Institute.

physical_eligibility.details: The competition level for BRO GREF Recruitment 2026 is expected to be moderate to high. The recruitment is restricted to Male candidates only, which reduces the applicant pool compared to open-gender recruitments. However, 899 vacancies across technical/trade posts will attract a large number of ITI-qualified and Matriculation-level candidates, especially for high-vacancy posts like Store Keeper Technical (300) and Operator Communication (261). The written exam followed by PET and Trade Test provides multiple elimination stages, but candidates with relevant ITI trade certificates and physical fitness will have a clear advantage. Ex-Servicemen and Ex-Agniveers get significant age relaxation and weightage, which makes certain trades more competitive in that segment. | The key differentiator here is the offline application process - many eligible candidates may miss out due to postal delays or incorrect application format, effectively reducing actual competition at the written exam stage. Candidates who submit complete and correct applications well before the deadline and prepare trade-specific written exam syllabi will have a significant edge.

how_to_apply: Candidates shall apply offline only; no online application mode is available. Application form and admit card must be downloaded from the official BRO website (www.bro.gov.in) and filled in English or Hindi only, in capital letters. The application form must be printed on A4 size 75 GSM bond paper (one side only) and admit card printed on both sides of a single paper. Candidates must first pay the application fee online via SBI Collect portal and attach the e-receipt copy. Candidates applying for more than one post must send separate applications with separate fee in separate envelopes. Candidates are strongly advised to send applications well before the closing date and not wait for the last day - GREF Centre will not be responsible for postal delays.
Candidates should read the detailed instructions and guidelines available at https://bfsissc.com before applying.
Documents Required to be Enclosed with Application Form:

Recent colour passport-size photograph (not older than 1 month from the date of Advertisement) pasted at the prescribed place on application form and admit card - minimum 12 copies of the same photograph to be carried throughout the recruitment process
Left hand thumb impression at the prescribed place on application form and admit card
Signature of candidate at the prescribed place
Copy of e-receipt of application fee paid via SBI Collect

Self-attested photostat copies of certificates for essential educational/technical qualifications (Matriculation onwards), date of birth, caste certificate (as per format in Appendix A/B), OBC NCL declaration (Appendix D), EWS certificate (Appendix C), Disability Certificate (Appendix E for PwBD), experience certificate (if applicable), Driving Licence copy (for OEM post)
NCC certificate copies (B or C grade), sports certificate, or relationship certificate for Sons of Serving/Ex-GREF personnel - for claiming weightage marks
Proof of residence/domicile (for correct physical standards region)
Any certificate in language other than Hindi or English must be enclosed with an attested Hindi/English translation by a Group A Gazetted Officer
Send Application (by Postal Service) to: Commandant, GREF Centre, Dighi Camp, Pune - 411015

Printout / physical application is NOT to be sent anywhere else. No hard copy of admit card for written exam will be sent by post - admit card will be posted to the communication address given by the candidate in the admit card.
For queries: Visit https://www.bro.gov.in - all recruitment updates, results, and call letters will be displayed on the BRO website only.

content_sections.heading: BRO GREF Recruitment 2026 - Vacancy Details
content_sections.text: A total of 899 vacancies have been announced across 6 posts/trades in General Reserve Engineer Force. Vacancies are distributed category-wise (UR/SC/ST/OBC/EWS) with horizontal reservation for Ex-Servicemen (ESM) and Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD). The number of vacancies is liable to increase/decrease/cancel at any stage without further notice. PwBD (Persons with Benchmark Disabilities) - Eligible Posts and Categories Abbreviations: OH - Orthopedically Handicapped, VH - Visually Impaired, HH - Hearing Impaired; S=Sitting, ST=Standing, W=Walking, BN=Bending, L=Lifting, RW=Reading & Writing, MF=Manipulation by Fingers, PP=Pulling & Pushing, SE=Seeing, C=Communication, KC=Kneeling & Crouching.

tables.caption: PwBD (Persons with Benchmark Disabilities) - Eligible Posts and Categories

rows.physical_requirement: S, ST, W, BN, MF, SE, RW, C
S, ST, W, BN, MF, SE, RW, C, L, PP, KC

content_sections.heading: BRO GREF Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the closing date of submission of application. Only Indian National Males are eligible to apply. Candidates awaiting results of final examination need not apply. All educational/technical qualifications must be from recognized University/Institute.

rows.essential_educational_technical_qualific: (i) 10+2 with Science subjects from a recognized Board; AND (ii) Two years Certificate in Architecture or Draughtsmanship from a recognized Institute, OR National Trade Certificate for Draughtsman (Civil) with one year practical experience in the trade
(i) 10+2 from a recognized Board; (ii) Typing speed of 30 words per minute in Hindi on computer (9000 KDPH at 5 key depressions per word)

Essential: (i) 10+2 from a recognized Board; (ii) Store keeping knowledge of vehicles or engineering equipment. Desirable: Three years experience in stores establishment, OR Class II Course for Storeman Technical as per Defence Service Regulation

(i) Matriculation from a recognized Board; (ii) Wireless Operator or Radio Mechanic Certificate from ITI, OR Defence Trade Certificate from Army Institute with knowledge of Radio Technology, OR Class I Course for Wireless Operator and Key Board as per Defence Service Regulations

(i) Matriculation from a recognized Board; (ii) Heavy motor vehicle driving licence or Driving license for Dozer/Excavator with six months experience in driving Dozer/Excavator, OR Class II Course of Operator Excavating Machinery as per Defence Service Regulations

(i) Matriculation from a recognized Board; (ii) Certificate of Auto Electrician from ITI or equivalent with one year experience as Electrician, OR Electrician Class 2 Course as per Defence Service Regulations

content_sections.heading: BRO GREF Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit
content_sections.text: Age limit for all posts/trades is between 18 to 27 years as on 01 Jun 2026. Age relaxation is applicable as per Government of India guidelines for various categories. Government servants get relaxation up to 40 years (UR) and 45 years (SC/ST). PwBD candidates get 10 years relaxation in upper age limit.

rows.category: Central Govt Civilian employees (min 3 years continuous regular service)

rows.age_limit_relaxation: Deduction of military service period from actual age + 3 years (in addition to OBC/SC/ST relaxation as applicable)
3 years relaxation (first batch of Agnipath Scheme: 5 years)

content_sections.heading: BRO GREF Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee
content_sections.text: Candidates must pay the non-refundable application fee online through the SBI Collect portal only (https://www.onlinesbi.sbi/sbicollect/icollecthome.htm?corpID=1232156) in favour of Commandant, GREF Centre, Pune-411 015. No other mode of payment will be accepted. A copy of the e-receipt must be attached with the application form.

content_sections.heading: BRO GREF Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process
content_sections.text: All candidates will undergo the recruitment process in the following sequence: (i) Written Examination, (ii) Physical Efficiency Test (PET) including physical measurements and Trade Test (TT) where applicable, (iii) Primary Medical Examination (PME) and Document Verification (DV), and (iv) Induction/Joining. The PET and TT are qualifying in nature - final merit is based solely on written exam marks, subject to qualifying PET, TT, and clearing PME. Minimum passing marks in written exam: 50% for UR/EWS/OBC; 40% for SC/ST. Trade test requires minimum 40% marks to qualify. Physical Efficiency Test (PET): Candidate must complete 1.6 km (1 mile) run in 10 minutes. PwBD, ESM, Ex-Agniveers, and departmental re-appointment candidates are exempt from PET. Physical Standards (Region-wise):

content_sections.heading: Competition Level Analysis for BRO GREF Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: The competition level for BRO GREF Recruitment 2026 is expected to be moderate to high. The recruitment is restricted to Male candidates only, which reduces the applicant pool compared to open-gender recruitments. However, 899 vacancies across technical/trade posts will attract a large number of ITI-qualified and Matriculation-level candidates, especially for high-vacancy posts like Store Keeper Technical (300) and Operator Communication (261). The written exam followed by PET and Trade Test provides multiple elimination stages, but candidates with relevant ITI trade certificates and physical fitness will have a clear advantage. Ex-Servicemen and Ex-Agniveers get significant age relaxation and weightage, which makes certain trades more competitive in that segment. The key differentiator here is the offline application process - many eligible candidates may miss out due to postal delays or incorrect application format, effectively reducing actual competition at the written exam stage. Candidates who submit complete and correct applications well before the deadline and prepare trade-specific written exam syllabi will have a significant edge.

content_sections.heading: Who should NOT apply for BRO GREF Recruitment 2026?
content_sections.text: Female candidates - this recruitment is strictly for Indian National Males only. Candidates who do not possess the requisite educational/technical qualification as on the closing date of application; those awaiting final exam results are not eligible. Candidates outside the age band 18-27 years as on 01 Jun 2026 (except those eligible for applicable category-based relaxation). OBC candidates from the Creamy Layer - they are not entitled to OBC reservation or age relaxation and must apply as UR/General category. Candidates who cannot meet the region-wise physical standards (height, chest, weight) as physical fitness is mandatory for induction. Candidates with permanent medical conditions listed as grounds for permanent rejection (colour blindness for Drivers/Operators, cardiac conditions, gross myopia, etc.) who cannot pass the Primary Medical Examination at GREF Centre. Candidates unwilling or unable to be posted anywhere in India, especially in remote, high-altitude, or difficult terrain areas in Northern and Eastern regions. Candidates who cannot arrange and send a physical application form with photograph, thumb impression, signature, fee e-receipt, and attested copies by post to Pune - there is no online application mode. Candidates with tattoos on any body part other than the inner forearm (inside elbow to wrist) or reverse palm - tattoos on other parts lead to disqualification at PME. Ex-servicemen who have already joined Govt civil service using ESM benefits and are claiming ESM status again - such candidates are treated as civilian employees.

content_sections.heading: Common mistakes / What to check before applying BRO GREF Recruitment 2026?
content_sections.text: Not printing the application form on A4 size 75 GSM bond paper (one side only) and admit card on both sides of a single paper as mandated - non-standard paper leads to rejection. Applying without attaching the e-receipt copy of the application fee paid via SBI Collect; no other payment mode is accepted and missing receipt causes rejection. Sending the application from a wrong/old address instead of present postal address - applications posted from wrong address are rejected to prevent fraudulent claims of 60-day receipt benefit. Not superscribing the envelope with "APPLICATION FOR (NAME OF TRADE/POST) Under Category (UR/SC/ST/OBC/EWS/PwBD/ESM/CPL)" as required - unlabelled envelopes may be misdirected. Mismatch between name in application and name in Matriculation certificate - even a dropped or added part of the name requires a Gazette Notification; otherwise candidature is summarily cancelled. Submitting multiple applications for the same post with different details or photographs - this results in lifetime debarment from all GREF examinations. OBC/EWS certificate dated older than one year from the closing date - OBC and EWS certificates must be fresh (not older than one year as on closing date) and in the prescribed Central Govt format. Candidates applying for more than one post/trade using the same envelope - each post requires a separate application with separate fee in a separate envelope. Not carrying the minimum 12 passport-size photographs (same as pasted on application and admit card) to all subsequent recruitment stages - shortage of photos causes disruption at PET/TT/PME stages. Ignoring the trade-specific syllabus and treating the written exam as a general knowledge test - each post has a distinct subject-wise syllabus and candidates must prepare trade-specific topics.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for BRO GREF Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates shall apply offline only; no online application mode is available. Application form and admit card must be downloaded from the official BRO website (www.bro.gov.in) and filled in English or Hindi only, in capital letters. The application form must be printed on A4 size 75 GSM bond paper (one side only) and admit card printed on both sides of a single paper. Candidates must first pay the application fee online via SBI Collect portal and attach the e-receipt copy. Candidates applying for more than one post must send separate applications with separate fee in separate envelopes. Candidates are strongly advised to send applications well before the closing date and not wait for the last day - GREF Centre will not be responsible for postal delays. Candidates should read the detailed instructions and guidelines available at https://bfsissc.com before applying. Documents Required to be Enclosed with Application Form: Send Application (by Postal Service) to: Commandant, GREF Centre, Dighi Camp, Pune - 411015 Printout / physical application is NOT to be sent anywhere else. No hard copy of admit card for written exam will be sent by post - admit card will be posted to the communication address given by the candidate in the admit card. Application form available at: https://www.bro.gov.in For queries: Visit https://www.bro.gov.in - all recruitment updates, results, and call letters will be displayed on the BRO website only.

faq.question: Q1. How many vacancies are available in BRO GREF Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 899 vacancies are available across 6 trades: Store Keeper Technical (300), Operator Communication (261), Operator Excavating Machinery OEM (207), Electrician (79), Draughtsman (42), and Hindi Typist (10), with 134 ESM and 15 PwBD seats.

faq.question: Q2. What is BRO GREF Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: BRO GREF Recruitment 2026 is a recruitment drive by Border Roads Organisation, General Reserve Engineer Force under Ministry of Defence, Government of India, under Advertisement No. 01/2026 to fill 899 posts across 6 trades/posts for Indian National Males only.

faq.question: Q3. What is the eligibility for BRO GREF Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Eligibility varies by trade: Draughtsman requires 10+2 Science plus 2-year Architecture/Draughtsmanship certificate; SKT/Hindi Typist require 10+2; Operator Communication/OEM/Electrician require Matriculation plus relevant ITI certificate or Defence Trade Certificate with experience. Only Indian National Males aged 18-27 years are eligible.

faq.question: Q4. What is the last date to apply for BRO GREF Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date for receipt of offline applications is 04 July 2026 for most states, and 19 July 2026 for candidates from NE States (Assam, Meghalaya, Arunachal Pradesh, Mizoram, Manipur, Nagaland, Tripura, Sikkim), Ladakh, remote HP areas, and Andaman & Nicobar Islands.

faq.question: Q5. What is the selection process for BRO GREF Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The selection process includes Written Examination, Physical Efficiency Test (1.6 km run in 10 minutes), Trade Test (where applicable), Primary Medical Examination (PME), and Document Verification. Final merit is based solely on written exam marks (minimum 50% for UR/EWS/OBC, 40% for SC/ST) subject to qualifying PET, TT, and PME.

faq.question: Q6. What is the age limit for BRO GREF Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Age limit is 18 to 27 years as on 01 June 2026, with relaxation of 5 years for SC/ST, 3 years for OBC, 10 years for PwBD, up to 40 years for Central Govt employees (UR), and deduction of military service period plus 3 years for Ex-Servicemen.

faq.question: Q7. Is there any application fee for BRO GREF Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Yes, application fee is ₹50 (non-refundable) for General/EWS/OBC/Ex-Servicemen/Ex-Agniveers candidates, payable online only through SBI Collect portal. SC/ST/PwBD candidates are exempted from fee payment.

faq.question: Q8. What is the salary for BRO GREF posts 2026?
faq.answer: Draughtsman receives Pay Band-1 (₹5,200-20,200) plus Grade Pay ₹2,800; all other posts receive Grade Pay ₹1,900, along with DA, HRA, Transport Allowance, Hard & Risk Allowance as per Government of India rules.

faq.question: Q9. Can female candidates apply for BRO GREF Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: No, this recruitment is strictly for Indian National Males only. Female candidates are not eligible to apply for any of the 6 trades/posts advertised under this notification.

faq.question: Q10. How to apply for BRO GREF Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must apply offline only by downloading the application form from bro.gov.in, paying ₹50 fee via SBI Collect portal, filling the form on A4 75 GSM bond paper, attaching e-receipt and self-attested documents, and sending by post to Commandant, GREF Centre, Dighi Camp, Pune-411015 by 04 July 2026.
